compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
DSO prior art + design context (from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md G-6). DSO's digital-service-orchestra classifies a new epic against ALL other open/in-progress epics with a FOUR-TIER severity ladder: benign / consideration / ambiguity / conflict. Its false-positive discipline is the transferable part and maps directly onto this epic's 'low false-flagging' success criterion:

- A shared resource must be a SPECIFIC, NAMED ENTITY (a named config key / schema / table / file region), not a vague 'both touch auth'.
- Two tickets touching the SAME FILE is BENIGN by default. Only SEMANTIC OVERLAP IN SUB-FILE REGIONS counts as contention. DSO's rule: 'disjoint-region edits are merge-order coordination, not shared-resource contention, and should be classified benign.' This is exactly why deterministic file-impact intersection (next_batch) is necessary-but-not-sufficient and why the LLM semantic tier is the delta this epic adds.

Suggested staged build (matches rebar's DET->LLM seam):
- Stage 1 (DET, no LLM): intersect this ticket's file_impact against other open/in_progress tickets' file_impact -- REUSE next_batch's existing conflict-detection primitive rather than reimplementing. Emit a candidate naming overlapping tickets.
- Stage 2 (LLM): feed ONLY the overlapping pairs to a semantic classifier applying DSO's named-resource + sub-file-region rules and the four-tier ladder. Route: benign -> log; consideration -> suggest an AC naming the shared resource as a falsifiable integration constraint; ambiguity -> surface for human; conflict -> strongest suggestion (candidate depends_on/duplicates/supersedes for human confirmation, never auto-applied).

Architectural note confirmed in design review: this stays OUTSIDE the plan-review gate (a per-ticket reviewer cannot see the whole store) -- consistent with this epic's existing scope statement. The plan-review gap report's G-6 'adopt' recommendation is therefore satisfied HERE, not by expanding the 5fd2 gate.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
PLAN OF RECORD (converged design, brainstorm 2026-07-08) — supersedes the staged-build sketch in the earlier DSO-prior-art comment on stage-1 mechanism.

## Where it runs
A store-wide overlap step invoked DURING `review_plan` (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:352), firing before claim — the tolerant, seconds-OK window. It is a STORE-WIDE step (this ticket vs. the store), NOT a per-ticket criterion, which reconciles with this epic's "outside the plan-review gate" scope: same window, wider data scope. It is ADVISORY ONLY — it never blocks the claim.

## Retrieval pipeline (two-stage retrieve-then-rerank; grounded in 2024-2026 IR/dedup research)
- Stage 0 — corpus representation (Cupid, arXiv:2308.10022): each ticket has an LLM-extracted digest {problem_keywords, component/area, key_entities, 2-6 atomic problem/repro propositions}; logs/traces/code discarded. Computed ONCE, async, cached in the SHARED store as a content-hash-keyed record on the ticket (same shape as the kind-keyed `attestations` map, reducer/_present.py:53) so validity is computed on read and content drift invalidates it. Shared = one client's enrichment is a cache-hit for all; no repeated billable calls. NO embeddings, NO vector DB, NO new dependency — reuses the Anthropic LLM rebar already has.
- Stage 1 — candidate generation (cheap, non-LLM): BM25F "More Like This" of the query ticket's digest against the corpus digests (title/problem-statement weighted high; max_doc_freq + stopwords prune boilerplate). Brute-force over a few hundred → sub-10ms, no ANN (unanimous research verdict: ANN only pays off >100K vectors; we are 100-1000x below). Take top ~20. EXCLUDE the query ticket's own graph (parents, children, siblings, and already-linked tickets).
- Stage 2 — precision judge (bounded LLM): pairwise judge over the ~20 candidates — both orderings aggregated (position bias), REQUIRED to cite the shared concrete artifact, DEFAULTS to "related-but-distinct" without one, abstains when unsure. Surfaces a small cap (~3) of candidate duplicates/supersedes/depends_on relations as ADVISORY findings, each with a ready-to-run `rebar link <A> <B> <rel>` command. Never auto-links.

## Query vs corpus (confirmed with product owner)
The 60-min soak governs the search CORPUS, not the query. The ticket completing plan review is NOT yet in the corpus (its own digest is enqueued on its cert, drained ≥60 min later). Its query is built at review time from its CURRENT FULL TEXT — one synchronous extraction piggybacked on the already-assembled plan-review context. Query-side digest vs corpus-side digests = apples-to-apples.

## Async enrichment queue (product-owner spec, realized on rebar's grain; grounded in broker-less-queue prior art)
- Queue = EVENTS in the shared store, keyed by ticket_id → "new certification replaces the old entry" is the natural latest-wins reduce (BullMQ debounce-mode / Huey put_if_empty). No SQLite/Redis/broker — rebar's event store already is a durable, shared, optimistically-concurrent queue substrate; a separate SQLite file could not live on a shared path nor be visible to all clients.
- Enqueue trigger: on plan-review CERTIFICATION, append an enqueue event with not_before = cert_time + 60min; a re-cert bumps not_before forward → the soak, done as a stored timestamp filtered at drain (deliberately NOT an in-process timer — those starve under continuous edits and don't survive short-lived processes).
- Claim: optimistic-append event (one drainer lands, losers retry) — rebar's existing concurrency primitive — plus a lease/reaper for crashes → at-least-once → summarization is idempotent (overwrite-by-content-hash).

## Drain execution — Tier 1 ONLY in v1
Opportunistic detached drain piggybacked on ordinary rebar invocations, git-gc-auto + npm-update-notifier style: cheap "is-drain-needed?" gate that no-ops, DETACH the LLM work to a child that outlives the command (reuse the existing async-push pattern, _store/push.py:108 — Popen(start_new_session=True, DEVNULL)), batch-cap tickets/run, lenient lock (skip if held), env opt-out. Zero client setup, no daemon, no broker, cross-platform.
Tier 2 (native scheduler registration) and Tier 3 (CI-scheduled drain) are SPUN OUT AS IDEAS — prove the system works daemon-free and CI-free first (many clients have no dedicated CI).

## Graceful-skip (no fallback)
If the [agents] extra is absent, plan-review is disabled, or an API key is absent, the whole feature GRACEFULLY SKIPS — no lexical-over-raw fallback. Consequence: a cold store surfaces little until drains have run; acceptable.

## Research grounding (subagent reports, 2026-07-08)
Two-stage retrieve-then-rerank + brute-force <1k + precision-in-stage-2: GitBugs arXiv:2504.09651, ZeroEntropy bi-vs-cross-encoder guides, arXiv:2505.16967 (cascading LLM judge). Cosine uncalibrated/anisotropic → rank only: arXiv:2504.16318, 2601.16907. Never LLM-read the corpus / lost-in-the-middle: Liu 2307.03172, NoLiMa 2502.05167 (effective length ≤~2K tokens). Cupid LLM-as-feature-extractor: 2308.10022. Bounded background work: git gc --auto / git maintenance, npm update-notifier. Broker-less queue: outbox / BullMQ debounce / Huey put_if_empty; event-sourced-append-beats-mutable-file for git stores.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body:
DERISKING EXPERIMENTS (2026-07-09) — results that validate the plan of record.

## 1. Recall (the load-bearing bet): does lexical BM25F over LLM Cupid-digests catch paraphrase WITHOUT embeddings?
Method: 8 seed tickets → heavy LLM paraphrases (minimal literal overlap) as queries; every ticket Cupid-enriched (JSON {problem_keywords, component_or_area, key_entities, propositions}); field-weighted BM25F over digests vs raw-BM25 baseline; measured at 60- and 200-ticket corpora.
Result:
- digest-BM25F: recall@10 = recall@20 = 1.0, MRR = 1.0 at BOTH scales (8/8 seeds at rank 1).
- raw-BM25 baseline: recall@20 0.875 (60) → 0.75 (200) — DEGRADES as distractors grow; digest holds perfect.
- Example: paraphrase "multiple independent signatures on same object being overwritten instead of accumulated" → digest rank 1, raw rank 49.
VERDICT: no-embeddings design is justified for RECALL at few-hundred scale. Caveats: N=8 (95% CI lower bound ~0.63 → "≥0.8 demonstrated", not "1.0 guaranteed"; a 30–50-query eval would tighten it). The residual risk is PRECISION at scale (query+doc digests share a model → vocab converges → near-dups converge too) — owned by S6's pairwise judge. If precision later bites, add a cheap re-rank, NOT an embedding index.

## 2. Latency: brute-force vs ANN.
BM25F single-query top-20, naive pure-Python: 0.17 ms @ 300 digests, 0.69 ms @ 800 (~14× under the 10 ms budget). VERDICT: brute-force is correct; no ANN / vector DB at this scale.

## 3. Enrichment economics.
Measured (Haiku 4.5): 727 in + 336 out tokens/ticket, ~3.4 s serial, $0.0024/ticket. Full store (793 tickets) ≈ $1.90 one-time, cached, re-run only on edit. VERDICT: amortized enrichment is cheap; shared-store cache pays for itself.

## 4. Store-primitive confirmations (code inspection).
- Manual write-lock `lock.acquire`/`LockHandle` + `txn.py::_acquire_write_lock` run ONE critical section across multiple appends → the queue's read-check-append claim AND atomic cert+enqueue are both achievable (S3 corrected: atomicity is possible, not impossible as an earlier draft claimed).
- `_engine_support/gates.py::_ticket_text` IS the canonical title+desc+comments normalizer → S2 reuses it for content-hash (drops the reinvented normalization that would have mis-hashed).
- Windows detach constants `DETACHED_PROCESS`/`CREATE_NO_WINDOW` are Windows-only (absent on POSIX) → S4 references them only inside an `os.name=="nt"` branch. API-derisked; not live-tested (no Windows host).

Experiment scripts/caches live under /tmp/cupid_exp/ (recall) and inline (latency); no repo files touched.

comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Epic implementation COMPLETE — all 7 child stories implemented, unit-tested, and pushed to Gerrit as a stacked change (changes 464-470), each with lint+typecheck+full-suite green (make test: 5017 passed). Stack order ee3d(464)->2d0f(465)->5a8f(466)->e1f4(467)->9022(468)->0f70(469)->c1de(470), rebased onto current origin/main, per-commit env-registry + module-size ceilings made self-consistent for per-change CI. Awaiting the two Gerrit gates (LLM-Review + Verified) on each change; will close each child after its Verified=+1 (bottom-first), then close the epic and submit so the stack lands together on main. Claim-model note: the enrichment queue claim is OPTIMISTIC append + earliest-live-lease arbitration (append_event self-serializes, so a claim cannot hold the write lock across its own append) — e1f4 + c1de descriptions updated to match.

compiled_state.description:
## Context
The plan-review gate (`5fd2`) reviews ONE ticket's plan in isolation. It deliberately does NOT detect SEMANTIC overlap, duplication, or undeclared dependencies BETWEEN SEPARATE tickets across the store. Existing coverage is partial and different in kind: `G4` checks consistency WITHIN one epic's children; `next_batch` schedules around DETERMINISTIC file-impact conflicts; `duplicates`/`supersedes` are MANUAL links. The residual — semantic overlap / duplication / hidden cross-ticket dependency across UNRELATED tickets — is uncovered.

## Scope
A store-wide overlap detector for the ticket UNDER PLAN REVIEW, comparing it against the whole store (cross-epic AND non-epic; any other ticket may overlap). It runs as a distinct STORE-WIDE step during the plan-review invocation window (before claim), NOT as a per-ticket `5fd2` criterion — a per-ticket reviewer cannot see the whole store, which is exactly why this is a separate step. It is ADVISORY ONLY and never affects the claim-gate verdict.

## Design (plan of record — full detail + research + experiment evidence in the comments)
Two-stage retrieve-then-rerank: (0) each ticket gets a cached, LLM-extracted Cupid digest (no embeddings, no vector DB); (1) brute-force BM25F over corpus digests narrows to ~20 candidates, excluding the query ticket's own graph; (2) a bounded LLM pairwise judge classifies each candidate and surfaces ≤3 advisory link suggestions for human confirmation. Enrichment is async via an event-sourced queue drained opportunistically (Tier 1). Child stories S1–S7 implement it; Tier-2/Tier-3 drain and a Jira-reconciler drain are spun out as linked ideas.

### Derisking experiments (run 2026-07-09 — see the experiment-results comment)
- **Recall (the load-bearing bet): VALIDATED.** digest-BM25F retrieved 8/8 heavy paraphrases at rank 1, recall@20 = 1.0, STABLE as corpus grew 60→200 (raw-BM25 baseline decayed 0.875→0.75). No embedding stage is needed for recall. Caveat: N=8 (≥0.8 demonstrated, not 1.0 guaranteed); the residual risk is PRECISION at scale, which the S6 judge is the mitigation for.
- **Latency: VALIDATED.** brute-force BM25F single-query top-20 = 0.69 ms at 800 digests (~14× under the 10 ms budget) → no ANN / vector DB.
- **Enrichment economics: VALIDATED.** ~$0.0024/ticket, ≈ $1.90 one-time for all 793 tickets, cached and re-run only on edit.
- **Store primitives: CONFIRMED.** the manual write-lock (`lock.acquire`/`LockHandle`, `txn.py` precedent) supports the queue's read-check-append AND atomic enqueue; `_ticket_text` is the canonical hash normalizer to reuse; Windows detach requires an `os.name` guard (API-derisked, not live-tested).

## Config placement (consistent across ALL children)
The single gate ENABLE flag `overlap_enabled` (bool) lives on `VerifyConfig` (`_config_schema.py` `[tool.rebar][verify]`, with its `_SECTIONS["verify"]` coercer). ALL other tunables (proposition bounds, BM25F weights, K, max_doc_freq, conf_threshold, surface_cap, soak, lease, drain mode/batch) are LLM-feature tunables and live on `LLMConfig` (`[tool.rebar.llm]`, `REBAR_LLM_OVERLAP_*`) — `_config_schema.py` reserves `llm.*` for that layer, so tunables must NOT go on `VerifyConfig`.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] For the ticket under review, overlap is detected against every non-graph-excluded ticket in the store (cross-epic AND non-epic), beyond deterministic file-impact and manual links — measured by the S6 labeled precision/recall fixture.
- [ ] Candidate `duplicates`/`supersedes`/`depends_on` relations are surfaced for HUMAN confirmation (each with a ready-to-run `rebar link` command); the tool NEVER auto-mutates links. Rationale for suggestion-only: link semantics require human judgment of intent, and a false auto-link corrupts the dependency graph that `ready`/`next_batch`/the completion gate depend on — a false suggestion costs one ignored line, a false auto-link costs silent graph corruption.
- [ ] Low false-flagging of legitimately-related-but-distinct work: precision is measured on a labeled fixture of ticket pairs (S6), and a related-but-distinct pair must not be surfaced. (This is the experiment-identified residual risk; S6 is its owner.)
- [ ] It is a store-wide step run during the plan-review window, advisory-only, never blocking or altering the claim-gate verdict.
- [ ] An ADR (docs/adr/) captures the architecture (Cupid-digest-without-embeddings, event-store-as-enrichment-queue, digest-as-REVIEW_RESULT-style-sidecar, overlap-at-review-time-not-as-a-5fd2-criterion) — DELIVERED in child S7 (precious-spy-hellbender).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects semantic overlap/duplication between separate tickets store-wide (cross-epic AND non-epic), beyond deterministic file-impact and manual links (delivered across S1–S7).
- [ ] Surfaces ≤3 candidate duplicate/supersede/dependency relations for human confirmation with copy-paste `rebar link` commands; does not auto-mutate links (S6, S7).
- [ ] Runs as a store-wide step during the plan-review window; advisory-only; does NOT run as a `5fd2` per-ticket criterion and does NOT affect the claim-gate verdict (S7).
- [ ] The architecture is captured in an ADR under docs/adr/ (owned by S7).
compiled_state.title: Cross-ticket overlap & dependency detection (store-wide: cross-epic + non-epic) — outside the plan-review gate
